required_skills: Embedded C and C++ programming for safety-critical real-time systems
Real-time operating system (RTOS) development and task scheduling
DO-178C software development assurance and software lifecycle compliance
Flight software architecture design including command and data handling (C&DH)
Software verification and validation including unit testing, integration testing, and MC/DC coverage
Hardware-software interface definition and register-level peripheral driver development
Fault detection, isolation, and recovery (FDIR) algorithm implementation
Memory management and deterministic execution in resource-constrained flight environments
Software configuration management (SCM) and version control in regulated environments
Requirements-based software development and traceability to system-level specifications
preferred_skills: Experience with NASA cFS (core Flight System) or GSFC mission frameworks
Knowledge of SPARK Ada for high-integrity software development
Familiarity with AUTOSAR or ARINC 653 partitioned software architectures
Model-based code generation using MATLAB/Simulink and Embedded Coder
Experience with spacecraft autonomy and onboard planning algorithms
Static analysis and formal verification tools (e.g., Polyspace, LDRA, VectorCAST)
Cross-compilation toolchains for RAD750, LEON, or ARM Cortex-M processors
frameworks: CMSIS (ARM Cortex Microcontroller Software Interface Standard)
responsibilities: Design and implement flight software subsystems for spacecraft or aircraft including telemetry processing, command handling, and mode management
Develop and integrate RTOS-based software on flight processors such as RAD750, LEON3, or ARM Cortex-M in C and C++
Execute DO-178C or NASA NPR 7150.2 software assurance activities including software development plans, test plans, and peer reviews
Implement and verify FDIR algorithms to detect hardware anomalies and autonomously recover spacecraft to safe operating modes
Write low-level hardware drivers for SpaceWire, UART, SPI, and I2C interfaces to communicate with avionics sensors and actuators
Achieve and document Modified Condition/Decision Coverage (MC/DC) for safety-critical software modules using VectorCAST or LDRA
Collaborate with GNC and systems engineers to integrate and validate guidance, navigation, and control algorithms in the flight software stack
Perform software-in-the-loop (SIL) and hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) testing to validate flight software behavior across nominal and off-nominal scenarios
Maintain rigorous software configuration management using Git and baseline control processes compliant with AS9100 or NASA standards
Support launch campaigns and on-orbit commissioning by analyzing flight telemetry and resolving anomalies in real-time software execution
